title: Sykli Pipeline
description: Canonical schema for Sykli pipeline JSON as emitted by the five SDKs (Go, Rust, TypeScript, Elixir, Python) and consumed by the engine. This schema describes the SDK-emitted shape and is intentionally strict (additionalProperties: false). The engine is currently more permissive — it ignores unknown fields and accepts a `condition` alias for `when` and a legacy list form for `outputs`. SDKs should not rely on those permissive paths. See docs/sdk-schema.md for the human-readable specification, contract gaps, and stability labels.

allOf.description: Agent-native semantic fields require pipeline version 3.

version.description: Pipeline wire-format/schema version. SDKs auto-detect: '3' if any executable task uses semantic fields such as task_type or success_criteria; otherwise '2' if any container, mount, dir, or cache resource is used; '1' otherwise.

tasks.description: List of task objects. May be empty.

resources.description: Map of resource id to resource definition. Only emitted by SDKs when v2 features are present (containers, mounts, dirs, caches).

resource.description: A directory or cache resource referenced by id from `task.mounts[].resource`.

globs.description: Optional glob patterns scoping a directory resource.

successCriterion.description: One declared success criterion. Criteria are conjunctive: all criteria in success_criteria must pass when engine checking is implemented.

task.description: A pipeline task (or review node when `kind == "review"`). The engine validates that non-gate, non-review tasks have a non-empty `command`; that rule is encoded in description rather than schema-level conditionals because gates and reviews compose with several other fields. SDKs validate cycles, duplicate names, and unknown dependencies; the schema does not.

allOf.description: `when` and `condition` are aliased by the engine (`when` wins). SDKs emit `when`. The schema rejects payloads that set both.
Review-only fields (primitive, agent, context, deterministic) are meaningful only when kind == "review". The schema rejects them on regular tasks (where kind is absent or not "review").
Review nodes are graph metadata nodes, not shell-command tasks. The canonical review-node contract rejects task execution fields.

name.description: Unique task name within the pipeline. Validated non-empty by the engine (validate.ex:115-128) and SDKs.

kind.description: Task kind. Default is task. The review kind is experimental.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Required for regular (non-gate, non-review) tasks; the engine raises `missing_command` otherwise (validate.ex:230-253). Gates and review tasks have no command.

task_type.description: Agent-native semantic class of an executable task. Requires top-level version == "3". Rejected on review nodes.

success_criteria.description: Declared verification metadata for executable task success beyond command completion. Requires top-level version == "3". Rejected on review nodes. Metadata only in Phase 3C-1; the engine does not evaluate these criteria yet.

allOf.description: At most one exit_code criterion is allowed per task.

env.description: Environment variables. Keys must be non-empty (validated SDK-side; engine accepts any).

inputs.description: File patterns used for input-based caching. Glob syntax.

outputs.description: Named output artifacts (output name → path). Engine also accepts a legacy list form which it normalizes into `output_0`, `output_1`, etc., but SDKs emit the map form canonically.

depends_on.description: Names of tasks this task depends on. Engine deduplicates (graph.ex:381) and validates references (validate.ex:159-185).

task_inputs.description: Cross-task artifact dependencies. Engine validates that `from_task` exists, `output` is declared on it, and the source task is a transitive dependency (graph.ex:766-801).

when.description: Conditional expression (e.g., `branch == 'main'`). Type-safe condition builders compile to this string.

condition.description: Deprecated alias for `when`. Engine accepts both via `map["when"] || map["condition"]` (graph.ex:382). SDKs should emit `when`.

secrets.description: Names of required secrets (v1-style; resolved from environment).

secret_refs.description: Typed secret references (v2). For source=vault, key must contain `#` separating path from field (e.g., `secret/data/db#password`). The `#` rule is enforced only by the Elixir SDK; engine and other SDKs accept any string.

matrix.description: Matrix dimensions; the engine expands the Cartesian product (graph.ex:522-577) into per-combination tasks named `<base>-<sorted-values-joined-by-dash>`. Dependent tasks are auto-rewritten to depend on every expansion.

services.description: Background service containers accessible by `name` as hostname.

mounts.description: Volume mounts (directory or cache resources, by id) into the container.

retry.description: Retry attempts on failure. SDKs emit only when non-zero.

timeout.description: Task timeout in seconds. SDKs emit only when set.

k8s.description: Kubernetes-specific options. Canonical shape is the flat 4-field structure {memory, cpu, gpu, raw}. The TypeScript SDK's K8sOptions interface declares additional fields (namespace, nodeSelector, tolerations, etc.) but does not serialize them; that is a TS-side type drift, not part of the contract.

memory.description: Memory request and limit (e.g., `4Gi`, `512Mi`). Validated by Go/Rust/Elixir/Python SDKs against Kubernetes quantity regex.

gpu.description: Number of NVIDIA GPUs to request.

raw.description: Pass-through string of raw Kubernetes JSON for advanced options (tolerations, affinity, etc.).

requires.description: Required mesh node labels for placement (e.g., `["docker", "gpu"]`).

provides.description: Capabilities this task provides. Each entry is a name with an optional value (which may be referenced by `needs` of downstream tasks).

needs.description: Names of capabilities this task requires. Engine auto-injects dependencies on whichever task provides each capability.

semantic.description: Semantic metadata used by the engine for smart task selection and AI context. Optional.

covers.description: Glob patterns of files this task validates. Used by smart selection.

intent.description: Free-form human description of the task's purpose.

select.description: Selection mode for smart task filtering.

gate.description: Approval gate. A task with a gate has no command; execution waits for approval according to the strategy.

strategy.description: Approval mechanism. `prompt` blocks on interactive input, `env` checks an environment variable, `file` polls a path, `webhook` waits for an HTTP callback.

timeout.description: Maximum seconds to wait for approval. Engine default 3600.

env_var.description: Required for strategy=env. Approval condition: env var is set to a truthy value.

file_path.description: Required for strategy=file. Approval condition: file exists.

webhook_url.description: Required for strategy=webhook. Approval condition: HTTP callback received.

verify.description: Reserved. Engine-readable (graph.ex:409) but no SDK currently emits this field. Likely values: `cross_platform`, `always`, `never`. Treat as unstable.

oidc.description: Reserved. Engine-readable (graph.ex:408) for OIDC credential binding but no SDK currently emits this field. Treat as unstable.

duration.description: Token duration in seconds. Engine default 3600.

history_hint.description: Engine-internal: populated by Sykli from prior runs (history_hint.ex:5). SDKs MUST NOT emit this field. Schema includes it for parser symmetry only.

primitive.description: Review-only. Identifier of the review primitive (e.g., `lint`, `security-scan`). Meaningful only when `kind == "review"`. Experimental.

agent.description: Review-only. Identifier of the agent fulfilling the review. Meaningful only when `kind == "review"`. Experimental.

context.description: Review-only. Files or context references provided to the review. Meaningful only when `kind == "review"`. Experimental.

deterministic.description: Review-only. Whether this review is deterministic. Engine defaults to false. Meaningful only when `kind == "review"`. Experimental.
